2604.08366.title: Scaling-Aware Data Selection for End-to-End Autonomous Driving Systems
2604.08366.paper_summary: Large-scale deep learning models for physical AI applications depend on diverse training data collection efforts. These models and correspondingly, the training data, must address different evaluation criteria necessary for the models to be deployable in real-world environments. Data selection policies can guide the development of the training set, but current frameworks do not account for the ambiguity in how data points affect different metrics. In this work, we propose Mixture Optimization via Scaling-Aware Iterative Collection (MOSAIC), a general data selection framework that operates by: (i) partitioning the dataset into domains; (ii) fitting neural scaling laws from each data domain to the evaluation metrics; and (iii) optimizing a data mixture by iteratively adding data from domains that maximize the change in metrics. We apply MOSAIC to autonomous driving (AD), where an End-to-End (E2E) planner model is evaluated on the Extended Predictive Driver Model Score (EPDMS), an aggregate of driving rule compliance metrics. Here, MOSAIC outperforms a diverse set of baselines on EPDMS with up to 80\% less data.
2604.08366.paper_summary_zh: 大型深度學習模型在物理人工智慧應用中依賴於多樣化的訓練數據收集工作。這些模型及其相應的訓練數據必須滿足不同的評估標準，以便在現實環境中可部署。數據選擇政策可以指導訓練集的開發，但目前的框架並未考慮數據點如何影響不同指標的模糊性。在這項工作中，我們提出了通過縮放感知迭代收集的混合優化（MOSAIC），這是一個通用的數據選擇框架，其運作方式為：（i）將數據集劃分為不同領域；（ii）從每個數據領域擬合神經縮放法則以符合評估指標；以及（iii）通過迭代添加來自最大化指標變化的領域的數據來優化數據混合。我們將MOSAIC應用於自動駕駛（AD），在此情境下，端到端（E2E）規劃模型在擴展預測駕駛員模型分數（EPDMS）上進行評估，該分數是駕駛規則合規性指標的總和。在這裡，MOSAIC在EPDMS上超越了多樣化的基準，所需數據量減少了多達80%。
2604.08366.author: Tolga Dimlioglu et.al.
2604.08366.authors: Tolga Dimlioglu, Nadine Chang, Maying Shen, Rafid Mahmood, Jose M. Alvarez
